name: Realistic Goals for Client-Server Computing
description: Aberdeen Group defines and evaluates the client-server computing paradigm across five critical planning areas: client hardware options, scalable server architectures, legacy system roles, systems software transitions, and network infrastructure. The study covers current client-server implementations and traces the evolution to advanced designs including distributed relational and object-oriented databases, cascading server-to-server transactions, and intelligent high-speed networks. Aberdeen concludes that client-server drives the next-generation computing vision but warns that the installed base technology does not yet fully support the vision and that significant non-technical barriers—management vision, process reengineering, and training—must be overcome.

creator.name: Peter S. Kastner, John Logan, Thomas Willmott
